bodyTail:
w-score 16-23, or sample first line 9 14.

Think broadly and implement, not just discuss: infer hidden test structure, exploit vertex-only Hausdorff, build recognizers, case-specific remeshing, parameterized families, or any legal structural breakthrough. Return only when you have a ready-to-submit submission_next.cpp file plus complete fallback code. If your UI cannot attach a file, say so explicitly and put the entire code block, but first try hard to provide the file.

Show more



ChatGPT said:
I’ll build a replacement file artifact, not just paste code. I’ll first mine the repo for the current best/failed signatures, then create and compile a new submission_next.cpp in /mnt/data before returning the link.

Received app response
The repository confirms the 81.938904 plateau and several bad families, so I’m not basing this on the previous inline star-removal draft. I’m moving to a hybrid: detect exact “safe-remesh” structures first, otherwise run a manifold-preserving independent-set vertex deletion with DP link retriangulation and conservative identity fallback.

Received app response
Thought for 7m 49s
